behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction.md explicitly lists all required string patterns tested: cachedPoint, cachedElement, cachedThreshold, cachedHit, WeakRef, pointsEqual, <=, version/versionNonce checks, result = hitElement || hitFrameName, cachedHit = result, return cachedHit.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction.md essentially dictates the implementation by listing exact code patterns like 'pointsEqual(point, cachedPoint)', 'cachedElement?.deref()', 'derefElement.version === element.version', 'result = hitElement || hitFrameName', 'cachedHit = result', 'return cachedHit' — this is the fix spelled out.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert exact string matches like 'cachedThreshold <= threshold', 'new WeakRef(element)', 'cachedElement?.deref()', 'const result = hitElement || hitFrameName' — alternative correct implementations using different variable names or equivalent logic (e.g., storing element id/version directly without WeakRef) would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All caching-related tests only perform substring checks via open().read() and 'X in content'; no test actually invokes hitElementItself to verify caching behavior (test_collision_module_imports is a smoke test, and repo CI tests don't specifically test caching behavior).

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub/no-op implementation wouldn't contain the required strings, so substring tests won't pass without editing collision.ts as specified.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All patterns described in instruction.md are checked in tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have descriptive names, docstrings, and a clear section separator for pass-to-pass tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction.md names the exact file 'packages/element/src/collision.ts' AND lists exact code strings that must appear, far beyond domain-obvious localization.
